req.title: Create patient (negative)
req.statement: A registered clinician shall create a patient resource with validated demographics; creation with missing mandatory elements shall be rejected. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-051 mandates that a clinician can create a Patient resource only when all mandatory FHIR demographics are present. The suite covers: (1) a positive EP case where a fully-valid payload yields 201; (2) a negative NEG/DT case where the 'name' mandatory element is omitted, expecting 422; (3) a boundary BVA case where 'name' is present but contains exactly one character (minimal non-empty string), probing the lower boundary of string-length validation; (4) a negative NEG case where a non-clinician role (e.g. 'patient') is denied, expecting 403; (5) a negative NEG case verifying that attempting to re-POST an already-existing patient with the same business identifier results in a conflict (409/422), covering the 'existing patient' conflicting state transition pathway called out in the error pathways. Note: test[4] was previously mis-labelled heuristic=STC which is reserved for positive state-transition coverage; since the duplicate-create scenario is an error/rejection pathway it is correctly classified as kind=negative heuristic=NEG.

tests.name: test_create_patient_valid_demographics_returns_201
preconditions: No patient with the test identifier exists
actions: Build a minimal but complete FHIR Patient payload with resourceType, name (family+given), birthDate, and gender
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-HLT-051 – positive path: valid demographics stored
FHIR R4 §2.1 Patient resource mandatory elements
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_patient_valid_demographics_returns_201(clinician_auth_headers, base_url, unique_identifier):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "identifier": [{"system": "urn:test", "value": unique_identifier}],
        "name": [{"family": "TestFamily", "given": ["TestGiven"]}],
        "gender": "male",
        "birthDate": "1990-01-15"
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Patient",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "Patient"
    assert "id" in body

tests.name: test_create_patient_missing_name_returns_422
actions: Build a FHIR Patient payload that omits the mandatory 'name' element
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains an OperationOutcome or error detail referencing the missing element
trace: R-HLT-051 – error pathway: 422 when mandatory element missing
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_patient_missing_name_returns_422(clinician_auth_headers, base_url, unique_identifier):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "identifier": [{"system": "urn:test", "value": unique_identifier}],
        "gender": "female",
        "birthDate": "1985-06-20"
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Patient",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "resourceType" in body or "error" in body or "issue" in str(body)

tests.name: test_create_patient_single_char_family_name_boundary
preconditions: System accepts non-empty strings for name.family
actions: Build a FHIR Patient payload where name[0].family is exactly one character ('A')
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP 201 Created (server accepts minimum-length family name)
OR HTTP 422 if the server enforces a minimum length > 1, surfacing the boundary behaviour
trace: R-HLT-051 – boundary: minimum string length for mandatory name element
BVA lower boundary for name.family field

tests.name: test_create_patient_non_clinician_role_returns_403
preconditions: A valid patient payload is prepared
actions: Build a complete FHIR Patient payload
trace: R-HLT-051 – error pathway: permission denial for non-clinician role
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_patient_non_clinician_role_returns_403(patient_auth_headers, base_url, unique_identifier):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "identifier": [{"system": "urn:test", "value": unique_identifier}],
        "name": [{"family": "Denied", "given": ["User"]}],
        "gender": "male",
        "birthDate": "1975-11-30"
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Patient",
        json=payload,
        headers=patient_auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"

tests.name: test_create_duplicate_patient_conflict_rejected
preconditions: A patient with a known unique identifier has already been created (setup step)
actions: POST /Patient with a complete valid payload to create the initial patient resource
Assert the first POST returns 201 to confirm setup succeeded
POST /Patient again with an identical payload whose identifier matches the already-existing patient
Capture HTTP response status and body from the second POST
expected: HTTP 409 Conflict OR 422 Unprocessable Entity on the second POST
Response body indicates a duplicate or conflicting-state error
The original patient resource is not altered
trace: R-HLT-051 – error pathway: conflicting state transition (duplicate patient identifier)
R-HLT-051 negative path: creation rejected when patient already exists with same business identifier
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_duplicate_patient_conflict_rejected(clinician_auth_headers, base_url, unique_identifier):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "identifier": [{"system": "urn:test", "value": unique_identifier}],
        "name": [{"family": "Duplicate", "given": ["Test"]}],
        "gender": "female",
        "birthDate": "1968-04-22"
    }
    first = requests.post(f"{base_url}/Patient", json=payload, headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert first.status_code == 201, f"Setup failed: {first.status_code} {first.text}"
    second = requests.post(f"{base_url}/Patient", json=payload, headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert second.status_code in (409, 422), (
        f"Expected 409 or 422 on duplicate create, got {second.status_code}: {second.text}"
    )
    body = second.json()
    assert "resourceType" in body or "error" in body or "issue" in str(body)
